analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The strongest technical setup on the board — a new-high cybersecurity leader working while high-beta tech breaks. TV BUY confirms.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Fortinet remains a rare software-plus-hardware security vendor that is both high-growth and durably profitable. Revenue is split between product (its FortiGate appliances/firewalls) and a faster-growing, higher-margin service/subscription stream (FortiGuard security services and support), with billings and deferred revenue the key forward tells. Operating margins run among the best in security software and free cash flow conversion is strong.
highlights: Revenue in the ~$6-7B range, high-single/low-double-digit YoY growth
Service revenue now the majority of the mix — recurring and high-margin
GAAP operating margin in the mid-20s%, non-GAAP well above
Robust free cash flow funding buybacks; net cash balance sheet
Billings growth the metric to watch as the forward demand signal

accomplishments: Established leadership in unified SASE and secure networking, converging firewall + SD-WAN + cloud security
Sustained one of the best margin profiles in the security-software group
Expanded OT/operational-technology security, a fast-growing adjacency

journey_52w.summary: A powerful re-rating over the past year: the stock roughly doubled off its base (+104% over three months alone) as firewall-refresh demand reaccelerated and the SASE story took hold, carrying it to fresh 52-week highs even as the broader semiconductor complex sold off on Middle East tensions.

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: Firewall refresh cycle reaccelerates; billings inflect higher

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: SASE/unified security momentum drives guidance higher

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to a new 52-week high, RSI a controlled 64 — un-extended leadership

risks: Earnings in ~17 days — a high-multiple name reacts hard to any billings miss
Product (appliance) demand is lumpier than services
Tech-tape guilt-by-association if the semi selloff broadens
watch: RSI staying below overbought on the breakout
critical.valuation: Premium multiple justified by best-in-class margins + reaccelerating growth; the risk is the multiple itself into earnings.

analyses.name: Northern Trust Corporation
analyses.verdict: Clean new 52-week high out of a tight VCP base with RS at the highs — the cleanest financials leadership setup. TV BUY confirms.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Northern Trust is a trust bank whose earnings lean on fee income (asset servicing and wealth management) plus net interest income on its balance sheet. Higher-for-longer rates lift NII, while assets under custody/administration (AUC/A) and assets under management drive the fee engine. Capital return via buybacks and a steady dividend is a core part of the story.
highlights: EPS growth strong (scan shows ~43% EPS growth), revenue +14%
Forward P/E only ~15 — inexpensive for the quality
Fee income (custody + wealth) the durable base; NII the rate kicker
AUC/A in the trillions — scale moat in asset servicing
Consistent buyback + dividend

accomplishments: Grew AUC/A to record levels on market appreciation and net new business
Improved operating leverage as expense discipline met higher fee + NII
Maintained a fortress capital position with steady shareholder returns

journey_52w.summary: A steady, un-hyped climb to new highs: the stock rode the higher-for-longer rate backdrop and a recovering fee base, building three tight contractions into a VCP base before breaking out this week with its relative-strength line already at the highs.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: NII benefits from elevated rates; fee income recovers with markets

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Operating leverage improves; guidance constructive

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to a new 52-week high out of a tight VCP base

risks: Q2 earnings in ~9 days — a near-term catalyst either way
A sharp rate cut cycle would compress NII
critical.valuation: ~15x forward earnings is cheap for a scale trust bank at new highs — valuation is a support, not a risk.

analyses.name: MetLife, Inc.
analyses.verdict: New high with RS at the highs; TV independently rates it STRONG BUY. A rate-levered insurance leader in the group the tape is rewarding.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: MetLife is a diversified life insurer earning a spread on a large, high-quality investment portfolio plus fee income from group benefits and asset management (MetLife Investment Management). Higher-for-longer rates lift new-money yields on the investment book, expanding spread income, while the group-benefits franchise provides steady, recession-resilient earnings.
highlights: Revenue base in the ~$70B+ range with strong spread income
+24% 3-month price momentum into a new high
Group benefits — steady, defensive premium base
Rising investment yields expanding net spread
Aggressive buyback shrinking the share count

accomplishments: Grew MetLife Investment Management (asset-management) AUM as a fee engine
Sustained best-in-class group-benefits margins
Returned substantial capital via buybacks and a growing dividend

journey_52w.summary: A methodical grind to new highs as the higher-for-longer rate regime kept lifting investment income and buybacks compounded per-share value; the defensive rotation of the past sessions accelerated the move.

milestones.label: H2 2025
milestones.note: New-money yields lift spread income

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Group benefits + investment income drive an earnings beat

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Prints a fresh 52-week high, RS at the highs

risks: A fast rate-cut cycle would compress spread income
Credit losses if the economy rolls over
critical.valuation: Trades at a modest multiple of book/earnings for a diversified insurer — reasonable at new highs given the rate tailwind.

analyses.name: Biogen Inc.
analyses.verdict: The cleanest large-cap healthcare setup — RSI 57, only 3.5% off its high, so early not extended. TV BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Biogen is a neuroscience-focused biotech whose legacy multiple-sclerosis franchise provides a large, high-margin (but slowly eroding) cash base, while newer launches — chiefly Leqembi (Alzheimer's, with Eisai) and the SMA/rare-disease portfolio — are the growth swing factors. The market is watching whether new-product ramp can offset MS erosion and turn the top line back to growth.
highlights: Revenue in the ~$9-10B range, inflecting as new launches scale
High gross margins typical of biotech
Leqembi (Alzheimer's) the key growth catalyst
Strong free cash flow funding pipeline + buybacks
+18% 3-month momentum as the growth narrative firms

accomplishments: Scaled the Leqembi Alzheimer's launch with partner Eisai — a landmark disease-modifying therapy
Diversified beyond MS into rare disease and immunology via pipeline and M&A
Maintained disciplined cost structure to protect margins during the MS transition

journey_52w.summary: A recovery year: after the multi-year drift on MS erosion, the stock based and then turned up as Leqembi's commercial ramp and pipeline optionality reframed the story from decline to re-acceleration, carrying it back toward 52-week highs.

milestones.note: Leqembi launch scales; MS erosion moderates

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: New-product revenue offsets legacy decline

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Within 3.5% of its high, RSI a controlled 57 — early in the move

risks: MS-franchise erosion could outpace new launches
critical.valuation: Inexpensive on earnings for a large-cap biotech — the debate is durability of the turnaround, not the multiple.

analyses.name: CVS Health Corporation
analyses.verdict: New high on a +37% 3-month turnaround; TV rates it STRONG BUY. A defensive healthcare re-rating.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: CVS Health is an integrated healthcare company spanning retail pharmacy, a pharmacy-benefit manager (Caremark), and health insurance (Aetna). The story of the past year has been margin recovery in the Aetna/medical-benefits segment after elevated utilization pressured earnings, alongside steady PBM and pharmacy cash flows. It's a high-revenue, lower-margin business where medical-loss-ratio trends drive the stock.
highlights: Revenue enormous (~$370B+ scale) across pharmacy, PBM and insurance
+37% 3-month momentum as the margin-recovery narrative took hold
Aetna medical-loss-ratio the swing factor on earnings
Strong operating cash flow supporting the dividend + deleveraging
TV STRONG BUY — technicals confirm the fundamental turn

accomplishments: Executed a margin-recovery plan in the health-benefits segment after utilization spikes
Integrated care-delivery assets (clinics/home health) into the model
Maintained scale leadership across pharmacy and PBM

journey_52w.summary: A striking round trip from pessimism to leadership: the stock was left for dead on medical-cost overruns, then re-rated sharply (+37% in three months) as management demonstrated the benefits margin was recovering, breaking out to a new high just as investors sought defensive healthcare.

milestones.label: Early 2025
milestones.note: Medical-cost pressure and guidance cuts pressure the stock

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: Cost-trend management shows margin stabilization

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to a new high, RSI 65 — healthy, not extended

critical.valuation: Still inexpensive on normalized earnings despite the run — the re-rating has room if margin recovery holds.

analyses.name: Eli Lilly and Company
analyses.verdict: Mega-cap GLP-1 leader basing ~4% off its high with RSI 64 — the premier secular grower in healthcare. TV BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Eli Lilly's story is dominated by its incretin franchise — Mounjaro (diabetes) and Zepbound (obesity), both tirzepatide — which is scaling into one of the largest drug categories in history, alongside a broad pipeline (Alzheimer's, immunology, oncology). Revenue growth is exceptional for a mega-cap pharma, and the constraint has been manufacturing capacity rather than demand.
highlights: Revenue growing rapidly (well into double digits) on incretin volume
Zepbound/Mounjaro the largest growth driver in pharma
+28% 3-month momentum, tight base near all-time highs
Massive capacity expansion to meet GLP-1 demand
High margins and reinvestment into a deep pipeline

accomplishments: Built the leading incretin (GLP-1/GIP) franchise for diabetes and obesity
Expanded manufacturing capacity aggressively to relieve supply constraints
Advanced an oral GLP-1 and next-gen obesity candidates through the pipeline

journey_52w.summary: A year of secular-growth leadership punctuated by volatility around competitive and pricing headlines; the stock consolidated its enormous multi-year gains into a tight base just below its highs, with RSI resetting to a healthy 64 — a constructive pause, not a breakdown.

milestones.note: Zepbound/Mounjaro scale; capacity constraints ease

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Pipeline read-outs (oral GLP-1, next-gen obesity) advance

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Tight base ~4% off the high, RSI 64 — coiling

risks: Very high share price — size carefully
GLP-1 competitive intensity (Novo and new entrants)
watch: Incretin script trends and share vs Novo
critical.valuation: Premium multiple, but justified by the growth rate and category size — a high-quality-growth core holding.

analyses.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
analyses.verdict: At a 52-week high out of a tight base — a defensive-consumer leader working while the tape is nervous. TV BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Monster is a pure-play energy-drink company with category-leading share, high gross margins, pricing power, and a net-cash balance sheet. Growth comes from international expansion, innovation (new flavors/formats), and price/mix, with the Coca-Cola distribution relationship providing global reach. Aluminum/freight costs and FX are the main margin swing factors.
highlights: Revenue growing high-single/low-double digits on price + international
Best-in-class gross margins for a beverage company
Net-cash balance sheet, consistent buybacks
+29% 3-month momentum into a new high
Low-beta ballast — exactly what a jittery tape wants

accomplishments: Sustained global energy-drink category leadership
Expanded international distribution via the Coca-Cola system
Held premium margins through cost inflation via pricing power

journey_52w.summary: A steady, low-drama climb to new highs: Monster kept compounding on international growth and pricing, and its defensive, low-beta profile drew flows as investors rotated out of high-beta tech into consumer staples over the past sessions.

milestones.note: International expansion and pricing drive revenue growth

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Margins recover as input costs normalize

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Sits at a 52-week high out of a tight base

critical.valuation: Premium staple multiple, but earned by growth + margins; a defensive-growth ballast holding.

analyses.name: EOG Resources, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The energy pick the tape is handing you — Hormuz risk is lifting oil and TV independently rates it STRONG BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: EOG is a premier low-cost US shale E&P known for capital discipline, high-return 'premium' drilling inventory, and a fortress balance sheet. Earnings and cash flow track oil/gas prices, but EOG's low breakeven and shareholder-return framework (base + special dividends and buybacks) make it a best-in-class way to own the commodity with less balance-sheet risk.
highlights: Low-cost premium drilling inventory — high returns at modest oil prices
Net-cash or near-net-cash balance sheet — rare for an E&P
Base + special dividends and buybacks return most free cash flow
Directly levered to the oil bid from Strait of Hormuz risk
TV STRONG BUY — technicals and the macro tailwind align

accomplishments: Maintained one of the lowest cost structures among US shale producers
Returned the bulk of free cash flow via a disciplined dividend + buyback framework
Expanded high-return exploration plays while holding capital discipline

journey_52w.summary: A largely range-bound year tracking crude, now catching a fresh bid: the renewed US-Iran escalation and the threatened Strait of Hormuz blockade put a geopolitical risk premium back into oil, and EOG — the quality name in the group — is the cleanest beneficiary.

milestones.note: Capital discipline holds; free cash flow returned to holders

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: US-Iran escalation + Hormuz blockade threat lift oil

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: TV flips to STRONG BUY as energy leads the tape

risks: The oil bid reverses fast if tensions de-escalate
catalysts: Hormuz/Middle East risk premium in oil
watch: Any change to the return framework
critical.valuation: Cheap on free-cash-flow yield; the risk is the commodity, not the multiple — treat as a tactical energy tailwind trade.

analyses.name: Arista Networks, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The cleanest large-cap AI-networking setup — RSI 54, 3% off its high, base-not-chase even as semis break. TV BUY.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Arista sells high-performance data-center switching/networking built on a single software OS (EOS), with the cloud titans and large enterprises as core customers. The AI back-end buildout — high-speed Ethernet fabrics connecting GPU clusters — is a direct, multi-year revenue tailwind. Gross margins are high for hardware and the balance sheet is net-cash.
highlights: Revenue growing strongly on cloud + AI back-end networking
High (software-like) gross margins for a networking vendor
Net-cash balance sheet, consistent free cash flow
RSI only 54 — un-extended even as the group sold off
AI Ethernet fabric a direct beneficiary of GPU cluster buildouts

accomplishments: Won a large share of hyperscaler data-center switching
Positioned Ethernet as the fabric of choice for AI back-end networks
Sustained premium margins on a single-OS software model

journey_52w.summary: A strong up-year on the AI-networking thesis, with sharp volatility whenever hyperscaler capex expectations wobbled; the stock has pulled back a few percent from its high and reset RSI to 54 — a constructive base while the broader semiconductor complex broke down on Middle East risk.

milestones.note: AI back-end Ethernet demand ramps; cloud capex strong

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Guidance reflects AI networking momentum

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Bases 3% off its high, RSI 54 — un-extended entry

risks: Guilt-by-association if the semi selloff broadens
Hyperscaler capex is lumpy and customer-concentrated
critical.valuation: Premium multiple for premium growth; the un-extended RSI makes the risk/reward favorable versus chasing extended peers.

analyses.name: Dominion Energy, Inc.
analyses.verdict: New high with RSI 67; TV rates it STRONG BUY. A regulated utility catching the defensive rotation with a data-center growth kicker.

financials.period: Last full fiscal year (FY ended Dec 2025)
financials.summary: Dominion is a regulated electric utility concentrated in Virginia — home to the largest data-center market in the world — giving it an unusual growth angle for a utility: surging power demand from AI/cloud data centers on top of a stable regulated rate base. Earnings are rate-base-and-allowed-return driven; the balance sheet carries typical utility leverage, and the dividend is a core part of the return.
highlights: Regulated rate base in Virginia — the top US data-center market
Data-center load growth an above-average demand driver for a utility
Steady allowed-return earnings + a substantial dividend
New high with RSI 67 as defensives lead
TV STRONG BUY — technicals confirm the defensive bid

accomplishments: Positioned its Virginia franchise to serve record data-center power demand
Advanced large regulated generation/transmission investment (including offshore wind)
Simplified the business back toward a pure regulated-utility model

journey_52w.summary: A steady re-rating as investors woke up to the data-center-power growth story layered on a defensive regulated utility; the defensive rotation of the past sessions pushed it to a fresh high as capital sought low-beta income.

milestones.note: Data-center demand narrative drives rate-base growth expectations

milestones.label: Q1 2026
milestones.note: Regulated investment program on track

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Prints a new high, RSI 67, as defensives lead

risks: Rate-sensitive — a yield spike on the oil move would pressure it
critical.valuation: Trades around a typical regulated-utility multiple, with the data-center growth angle arguably under-appreciated.
